approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies a recognised asset or liability as an eligible hedged item, and IAS 21 indicates that certain intragroup monetary items generate exchange differences that are not fully eliminated in consolidation.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 separately recognises highly probable forecast transactions, including qualifying intragroup foreign-currency transactions affecting consolidated profit or loss, as a distinct hedge-accounting model.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 establish net investment hedging as a separate top-level IFRS hedge-accounting model for foreign currency risk relating to a foreign operation.
